text:
There once was a little mouse named Mittens. Mittens lived in a cozy little burrow near some giant apple trees.

One crisp autumn morning, Mittens woke up to the sweet sound of falling apples. She sniffed the air and realized there was a big gala underway among the trees. She could hear the guests chatting and laughter filling the air. A majestic red squirrel, Sir Redford the Elder, was attending.

Mittens scampered over to investigate. She found Sir Redford sitting on an old branch, picking out the finest apples from his bounty. Mittens watched curiously. The squirrel noticed her and didn't mind at all.

"Good morning, Mittens," he said.

"Good morning! I heard there's a fantastic meal tonight," she replied, nodding towards the apples.

"I suppose there would be," Sir Redford smirked. "You have just the right talents. Nothing beats a fresh apple!"

With haste, Mittens scurried into the trees
